name: pylabrobot
tagline: Python SDK skill wrapping PyLabRobot to drive multi-vendor lab hardware (liquid handlers, plate readers, pumps, centrifuges) from a unified
verdict: No malicious, obfuscated, or hidden content found; the file is plain documentation of a real open-source SDK (PyPI: PyLabRobot). Main real-world risk: it directs code that moves physical robots (pipetting, centrifuging) and only recommends simulation-first as a best practice rather than requiring a confirmation gate before hardware runs, and it gives no guid

highlights.summary: Skill drives physical lab robots (pipette aspirate/dispense, centrifuge spin, heater/incubator temperature) which have real-world, irreversible effects (spills, sample loss, hardware collision). Best
No explicit statement of what the skill should produce as its final output/deliverable (e.g., a runnable protocol script vs. a report); output is only implied by the code examples.

Failure handling is limited to one bullet, 'Implement proper async error handling for hardware operations' (line 127), with no guidance on specific hardware failure modes (tip-pickup failure, dispense
